authors: Chunqiu Steven Xia, Zhe Wang, Yan Yang, Yuxiang Wei, Lingming Zhang
title: Live-SWE-Agent: Can Software Engineering Agents Self-Evolve on the Fly?
storage_format: Custom tools as executable Python scripts created on-the-fly by the agent during issue resolution. They include file editors, code searchers, and language-specific analyzers (e.g. Go analyzer). Stored in the execution environment's filesystem; an average of 3.28 tools per task with reflection.
llm_used: Claude 4.5 Sonnet, Claude 4 Sonnet, Claude 3.7 Sonnet, GPT-5, GPT-5-Mini, GPT-5-Nano, Gemini 3 Pro
benchmarks_used: SWE-bench Verified (500), SWE-Bench Pro (731), SWE-bench Multilingual (50 subset)
performance_metrics: Resolve Rate (%), Average $ Cost per issue
baseline_compared: mini-SWE-agent (without tool creation), SICA, DGM, HGM (offline self-improving agents), SWE-agent (handcrafted tools)
memory_size_over_time: Tools created per-task; do not persist across tasks; ~3.28 tools/task with reflection
cost_per_task: $0.05 (GPT-5-Mini) to $0.73 (Claude 4.5 Sonnet, SWE-Bench Pro) per issue
main_result: Live-SWE-Agent reaches 77.4% resolve rate on SWE-bench Verified (Gemini 3 Pro) and 45.8% on SWE-Bench Pro (Claude 4.5 Sonnet), surpassing all existing agents without test-time scaling. On subset-60, it surpasses DGM (65.0% vs 53.3%) at zero offline cost.
performance_vs_baseline: +3.2pp vs mini-SWE-agent with Gemini 3 Pro (77.4% vs 74.2%); +4.8pp with Claude 4.5 Sonnet (75.4% vs 70.6%); +11.7pp vs DGM on subset-60 (65.0% vs 53.3%); +2.2pp vs SWE-agent on Pro (45.8% vs 43.6%)
cost_vs_baseline: Zero offline cost vs 1231 hours (DGM) and 512 hours (HGM); online cost comparable ($0.48 vs $0.46 Gemini 3 Pro; $0.68 vs $0.56 Claude 4.5 Sonnet)
limitations: Self-evolution limited to tool creation (does not modify prompts or workflows); tools do not persist across tasks; weak LLMs (GPT-5-Nano) degrade drastically with tool creation (-68.2%); reflection consumes additional tokens
validity_threats: SWE-bench Verified is predominantly Python; subset-60 inherited from prior work; comparison with DGM/HGM uses results reported with GPT-5-Mini; per-task cost depends on variable API pricing; tools discarded between tasks limit assessment of persistence
